evidence_excerpt: Further follow-up in 1997 showed gradual resumption of normal adrenal function, and from July onwards the patient received no hydrocortisone replacement. Blood pressure and glucose tolerance continued to be normal.

quality_of_life.evidence_excerpt: Hypertension resolved after surgery, and diabetes mellitus resolved after delivery; subsequent follow-up showed normal BP and glucose tolerance without ongoing symptoms.

timeline_events.evidence_excerpt: Post-operatively, secondary adrenal insufficiency ensued, necessitating continuing hydrocortisone replacement over 12 months.
Hormonal evaluation in the 23rd week of gestation, 6 weeks after operation, showed remission of the pheochromocytoma.
Diabetes mellitus resolved after the uncomplicated vaginal delivery at term.
Further follow-up in 1997 showed gradual resumption of normal adrenal function, and from July onwards the patient received no hydrocortisone replacement.

notes_on_uncertainty: The index time is approximated to early April 1996 when a preliminary diagnosis and treatment plan were established. Exact dates are not always provided; time periods are inferred relative to this diagnosis. Secondary adrenal insufficiency is labeled as a major complication due to its clinical significance and prolonged replacement need, though it is an expected consequence after suppression from autonomous cortisol secretion.
